title: Transferability and discriminating power of ISSR and SCoT markers for studying genetic diversity in Dacryodes edulis accessions from Nigeria
author: Oyebola, Temitope Omoniyi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Dacryodes edulis  is a culturally and economically important fruit tree native to Central and West Africa. Despite its benefits, its genetic diversity remains underexplored. This study has used Inter-Simple Sequence Repeat (ISSR) and Start Codon Targeted (SCoT) markers to assess the genetic diversity and relationships among 32 accessions collected from Nigeria. Genomic DNA was extracted using a modified CTAB protocol and amplified by 15 ISSR and 10 SCoT primers. The results highlight the effectiveness of these markers in elucidating genetic variation and phylogenetic relationships, thus providing a foundation for genetic resource management and conservation.
